services_section_subtitle: Specification, design, code, deployment, monitoring, on-call. One engineer for the whole stack.

service_3_bullet_3: Deployed on Kamal, monitored with Sentry
how_section_lede: I deliver tools you own and operate. You never become dependent.
how_point_1: Every delivery includes a walkthrough with the people who will actually use the tool, plus a user guide written in plain language.
how_point_2: Code, content, infrastructure, domain names: everything is yours from day one. No proprietary editor, no platform lock-in.
how_point_3: Plain-language README, architecture notes, deployment runbook. Any engineer can pick up where I left off.
how_point_4: Anything that breaks within the warranty window after launch, I fix at no extra cost. Defects are not change requests.
pricing_title: One price. One engineer. No surprises.
pricing_subtitle: Flat monthly fee. Cancel any month.
pricing_feature_2: Unlimited requests, one at a time
pricing_note_text: You message requests. I ship them, one at a time. No limits. No commitment.
personal_eyebrow: The engineer you'd actually work with
personal_credential: MSc Software Engineering, University of Oxford
personal_message_1: I've been writing production software for over ten years, primarily Ruby on Rails, plus everything that lives around it: Postgres, background jobs, integrations, deployment, on-call.
personal_message_2: Monégasque national, resident in the Principality. I work with Monaco SMEs because I live here and I know the local pain: software priced for global enterprises, agencies that send junior staff after they win the pitch, freelancers who disappear between projects. I don't subcontract. I don't fixed-price work I haven't scoped. I won't pretend I have capacity when I don't.
mission_quote: Every Monaco business deserves software designed around its operations, not the other way around.

contact_form_success: Message sent. I'll reply within a few hours.
privacy_meta_description: How laurentcurau.com handles your data. Short answer: barely any data, no cookies, no tracking beyond aggregated analytics.
privacy_intro: This is a one-person freelance site. It collects as little data as possible, and what it does collect is described below, honestly and in plain language.
privacy_who_body: This site is operated by Laurent Curau, independent software engineer, registered as a sole trader in Monaco (PR 01061), based in Monaco 98000. For any privacy-related question, write to hello@laurentcurau.com.
privacy_what_body: Nothing about you personally, unless you choose to message me. The site has no contact form, no account system, no newsletter, no third-party advertising, and no cookies.
privacy_analytics_body: The site uses Plausible Analytics, a privacy-friendly, cookieless analytics tool that records aggregated visit counts (pages viewed, country, referrer, browser type) without identifying individual visitors. No personal data is stored, no cross-site tracking, no advertising profiles. Plausible is hosted in the EU. You can read their data policy on plausible.io.
privacy_whatsapp_body: If you click a "WhatsApp" button on this site, you're handed off to WhatsApp (operated by Meta Platforms Ireland Limited). At that point you're subject to WhatsApp's own privacy policy. Nothing about that click is recorded on this site beyond the aggregated Plausible event.
privacy_rights_body: Under EU/Monaco data-protection law you have the right to access, rectify, or erase any personal data held about you, and to lodge a complaint with the supervisory authority. Since this site doesn't hold personal data on you, there's typically nothing to access or erase. But write to hello@laurentcurau.com if you want to check.
privacy_changes_body: If this notice changes meaningfully, the date at the top of the page will be updated. There's no notification mechanism: this site doesn't have your email.
terms_meta_description: Terms of use for laurentcurau.com, a static marketing site for an independent software engineering practice.
terms_intro: These terms cover the use of this website. They don't constitute a service agreement: that's negotiated separately and signed in writing.
terms_about_body: laurentcurau.com is the marketing site of Laurent Curau, independent software engineer, registered as a sole trader in Monaco (PR 01061), based in Monaco 98000. The site exists to describe the engineering services I offer and to show selected past projects.
terms_use_body: You're welcome to read the site, link to it, and contact me through the channels listed. The content (text, screenshots, code samples) is provided as-is, for information only, not as professional advice. Please don't republish or scrape the content without asking first.
terms_third_parties_body: The site links out to live client sites (afida.com, agencegaremonaco.com, cca-mc.com) and to WhatsApp, LinkedIn, and email. I'm not responsible for the content or behaviour of those external services.
terms_warranty_body: Information on this site is provided in good faith but without any guarantee of completeness or accuracy. The case studies describe projects I've worked on; specific outcomes for any future engagement depend on the engagement itself.
terms_law_body: These terms are governed by the law applicable in the Principality of Monaco.
terms_contact_body: Any question about these terms, or about working together, can be sent to hello@laurentcurau.com.

case_afida_meta_title: Afida — Custom Rails 8 B2B e-commerce | Laurent Curau
case_afida_meta_description: Custom Rails 8 e-commerce for eco-friendly packaging. Stripe, scheduled reorders, branded-product configurator, Google Merchant feed. Trusted by 500+ UK businesses.
case_afida_description: Custom B2B e-commerce platform for Afida, a British eco-responsible packaging brand. Built and operated end to end by one person, from the Postgres schema to the Google Merchant feed.
case_afida_related_service_body: Stripe-powered B2B and B2C commerce, built end-to-end.
case_afida_section_1_body: Afida supplies eco-responsible food packaging to hospitality businesses across the UK. Its customers order in volume, come back at regular intervals, and expect from a B2B supplier what they know from B2C: a fast site, frictionless payment, an account that remembers everything.
case_afida_section_2_body: Generic e-commerce platforms model B2B poorly. Quantity-tier pricing, UK VAT rules that vary by product type, recurring orders and client-branded packaging don't fit comfortably in a Shopify theme. Every workaround adds a plugin, and every plugin adds slowness and one more dependency.
case_afida_section_3_body: A custom Rails 8 application, where every business rule is code I control rather than a plugin I endure. Payments run through Stripe Checkout: hosted payment pages, three shipping options, UK VAT calculated automatically. Regular customers schedule reorders: the order prepares itself, with a validation window before any charge. A branded-product configurator handles design upload and quantity tiers. A free sample request feeds lead capture. And a Google Merchant XML feed keeps the catalogue continuously exposed to Google Shopping.
case_afida_section_4_body: Rails 8, Hotwire for interactivity without JavaScript weight, Postgres, Stripe. One application, one deployment, no microservices to orchestrate: the stack is sized to the team that runs it.
case_afida_section_5_body: The platform is used daily by more than 500 British hospitality businesses, including Mandarin Oriental, Marriott and Hawksmoor. The site, SEO, email and analytics are operated by the same person who wrote the code: when something needs to change, there is nobody to convince between the request and production.
case_gare_meta_title: Agence de la Gare — Monaco real estate, 9 locales | Laurent Curau
case_gare_meta_description: Rails 8 multilingual real-estate site for Monaco's pioneer agency (founded 1942). 9 locales, Immotoolbox sync, Typst PDFs, multi-currency.
case_gare_description: Custom multilingual site for one of Monaco's pioneering real estate agencies, founded in 1942 and a member of the Chambre Immobilière Monégasque.
case_gare_section_1_body: Agence de la Gare sells and rents Monaco real estate to an international clientele. A clientele that arrives in nine languages, compares in several currencies, and expects clean brochures it can forward. The property portfolio, meanwhile, lives in Immotoolbox, the agency's line-of-business tool.
case_gare_section_2_body: Multilingual real estate sites usually fail on two points. First, translation: translating the interface is not enough, you need translated routes so each language gets indexed properly by search engines. Second, synchronisation: if the site doesn't mirror the business tool automatically, someone re-enters listings by hand, and the site ends up lying.
case_gare_section_3_body: A Rails 8 application whose i18n covers nine languages (FR, EN, IT, DE, SV, NO, DA, FI, RU) with translated routes: every property has a proper URL in every language. Districts, buildings and properties sync daily from the Immotoolbox API, orchestrated by Solid Queue: nobody re-enters anything. Every property generates its own PDF brochure, rendered with Typst, ready to send to a client. Prices display in multiple currencies backed by daily ECB rate updates, and the agency's YouTube channel feeds the site continuously.
case_gare_section_4_body: Rails 8, Hotwire, Postgres, Solid Queue for background jobs, Typst for PDF rendering, deployed with Kamal. No CMS to maintain on the side: the site is the application.
case_gare_section_5_body: The agency publishes once, in its own tool, and the site follows: nine languages, brochures and currencies included. An agency founded in 1942 with a web presence its younger competitors don't have.
case_cca_meta_title: Christian Curau Architecte — Editorial site + custom web FTP | Laurent Curau
case_cca_meta_description: Sober editorial portfolio for a heritage Monaco architect, plus a stateless web FTP/FTPS client for sharing CAD files securely without a desktop tool.
case_cca_description: Two deliverables for a Monaco architecture firm founded in 1984, Conservator of the Prince's Palace: an editorial portfolio and a custom file-sharing tool.
case_cca_related_service_body: Quiet software that sits behind your operations.
case_cca_section_1_body: An architecture firm lives off two things that digital tools serve poorly by default: showing its work faithfully, and exchanging large CAD files with its partners. The firm needed both, without superfluous complexity.
case_cca_section_2_body: A deliberately restrained Rails editorial portfolio, centred on project pages. Typography does the heavy lifting; the images breathe. The homepage display order is curated rather than chronological: the firm chooses what to put forward, the way one composes an exhibition.
case_cca_section_3_body: The second deliverable is an internal tool: CCA-FTP, an FTP/FTPS client accessible from any browser, built to share large CAD files securely without installing software. Its distinctive trait: it is stateless. No database to back up or leak; sessions live in an encrypted cookie, the protocol is auto-selected from the identifier, filenames are sanitised, and an invisible CAPTCHA blocks automated abuse. Fewer moving parts means less attack surface and less maintenance.
case_cca_section_4_body: Rails 8 for both applications, deployed with Kamal. Two distinct apps, each reduced to its essentials.
case_cca_section_5_body: The firm shows its work the way it intends and exchanges files without depending on a third-party service or desktop software. Two precise tools, sized exactly to the need, operated by the person who built them.
case_mdb_meta_title: Maison de Bacon — Visual redesign and performance remediation | Laurent Curau
case_mdb_meta_description: A full visual redesign of the Maison de Bacon website, a seafront dining institution on Cap d'Antibes. New identity, Roof Top page, Zenchef booking, plus a performance audit and remediation.
case_mdb_description: Visual redesign and performance remediation for a seafront gastronomic institution at Cap d'Antibes, open since 1948.
case_mdb_section_1_body: Maison de Bacon is an emblematic table of the French Riviera. Its bilingual FR / EN WordPress site carried years of successive additions: a dated visual identity, a menu that had become unreadable, and an accumulation of plugins weighing on every page load.
case_mdb_section_2_body: For a restaurant of this reputation, the website is the first dining room: it's where the guest decides to book. A slow, dated site costs reservations, silently. And on WordPress, slowness almost always has the same cause: too many plugins, a misconfigured cache, oversized images.
case_mdb_section_3_body: On the design side, a complete redesign in Elementor: a rethought homepage, a dedicated terracotta identity for the Roof Top, a restructured mega-menu that reads clearly again, verified Zenchef reviews, and a fixed booking button present on every page, in French and in English. On the performance side, an audit followed by its remediation: the plugin count went from 41 to 17, the WP Rocket cache was set right, and images are now compressed and converted to WebP automatically on upload.
case_mdb_section_4_body: WordPress, Elementor, WP Rocket, Zenchef. No forced technical rebuild: the client keeps the tool their team knows how to use, stripped of what was slowing it down.
case_mdb_section_5_body: A site that finally looks like the house it represents, with booking within reach on every page.
